title: Describing the People of Praia do Sol
description: Appearance + personality adjectives. Tall, short, kind, lazy — and the -ED / -ING adjective trap (bored vs boring).
summary_pt_br: Adjetivos de aparência e personalidade. Armadilha: -ED é o que EU sinto (I am bored — entediado), -ING é o que CAUSA o sentimento (The movie is boring — chato). Trocar muda tudo.

scenes.text_en:
Bia sketched her friends in her notebook.
'Mr. Kumar is TALL and KIND. His hair is GREY.
Rafa is MEDIUM-HEIGHT, FUNNY, a bit LOUD.
Joca is SKINNY, QUIET, almost SHY. His eyes are BROWN.
Dona Lúcia is SHORT, GENTLE, and her smile is WARM.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Bia desenhou os amigos no caderno.
'Mr. Kumar é ALTO e GENTIL. O cabelo é GRISALHO.
Rafa é de ALTURA MÉDIA, ENGRAÇADO, um pouco BARULHENTO.
Joca é MAGRO, QUIETO, quase TÍMIDO. Olhos CASTANHOS.
Dona Lúcia é BAIXINHA, DOCE, com um sorriso AQUECIDO.'

items.pt: muito magro / esbelto / acima do peso

scenes.title: -ED vs -ING adjectives — the classic trap
scenes.body_en: -ED describes how YOU feel. -ING describes what CAUSES the feeling. 'I am BORED' (I feel it). 'The class is BORING' (it causes it). Saying 'I am boring' means YOU are boring to others — probably not what you meant!
scenes.body_pt: -ED = o que você sente. -ING = o que CAUSA. 'I am bored' = estou entediado. 'I am boring' = EU sou chato (insultando você mesmo!). Não troque — muda totalmente o sentido.

examples.en: I'm excited. (me) — The trip is exciting. (cause)
examples.pt: Estou animado / A viagem é empolgante.

examples.en: She's tired. — The job is tiring.
examples.pt: Ela está cansada / O trabalho é cansativo.

examples.en: He's surprised. — The news was surprising.
examples.pt: Ele ficou surpreso / A notícia foi surpreendente.

exercise.question: Maya failed to read her book. Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: The book CAUSES boredom → -ING (boring). Maya FEELS bored (-ED).
exercise.hint_pt_br: Livro causa tédio → boring. Maya sente tédio → bored. Não confunda!

exercise.question: Complete: 'I'm ___ (interest) in English.'
exercise.explanation: INTERESTED = you feel it. INTERESTING = the subject causes it.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Sentimento pessoal = interested. 'English is interesting' = o inglês é interessante.

exercise.explanation: Five descriptors for people. Use them in sentences about characters you know.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Cinco descritores. Pratique usando com pessoas que você conhece.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Describing the People of Praia do Sol". Appearance + personality adjectives. Tall, short, kind, lazy — and the -ED / -ING adjective trap (bored vs boring). Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Describing the People of Praia do Sol". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
Describing the People of Praia do Sol
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Describing the People of Praia do Sol'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Describing the People of Praia do Sol".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.pt_hint: Regras de gramática + exercícios interativos gratuitos.
